rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Or Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 6, 2014 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Turner Construction Company¿s motion for summary judgment or, alternatively, summary adjudication: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:45a.m. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 6, 2014 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Pierce Engineering Company¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issue 2 (premises owner/contractor liability) and off calendar as to issues 1, 3 and 4 (strict liability, civil battery and punitive damages). Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that the sophisticated user defense applies to bar plaintiff¿s claims against it. Even if defendant demonstrated that plaintiff was a sophisticated user, such defense applies only to the duty to warn and is not a complete bar to plaintiff¿s claims. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant breached a duty owed to him. Plaintiff¿s responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Even if defendant had sustained its burden, Dr. Cohen¿s declaration would create a triable issue whether defendant breached a duty owed to plaintiff. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff has no evidence to support his premises owner/contractor liability cause of action. Defendant failed to show that such cause of action must fail even if plaintiff has no evidence that defendant exercised control over the premises and/or plaintiff¿s work. Plaintiff represents that he has dismissed his strict liability and civil battery causes of action and his claim for punitive damages. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: SHARON DUNN et al VS. AMCHEM PRODUCTS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication;
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 6, 2014 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Merlex Stucco, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. As to summary judgment and summary adjudication issues 1, 2, 5 and 6, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Charles Dunn was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Charles Dunn, Jr.¿s deposition testimony and defendant¿s failure to demonstrate that plaintiffs failed to provide meaningful responses to comprehensive interrogatories designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiffs have to support their contention that Charles Dunn was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to negate an essential element of plaintiffs¿ claims. Even if defendant had sustained its initial burden, the deposition testimony of Charles Dunn, Jr. and William Ruth, defendant¿s responses to standard interrogatories and Eugene Carpenter¿s declaration would create a triable issue whether Charles Dunn was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (Merlex stucco) attributable to defendant. As to summary adjudication of issues 3, 4 and 7, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence supporting their false representation and intentional tort causes of action and claim for punitive damages. Defendant¿s failure to demonstrate that plaintiffs failed to provide meaningful responses to comprehensive interrogatories designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiffs have to support these claims precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiffs¿ objections to Susan Verburg¿s deposition (Exhibit O) are sustained. All plaintiffs¿ other objections are overruled. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Carpenter¿s declaration would be overruled if the court reached plaintiffs¿ evidence. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
